extra.reasoning:
The monotonicity property (setting back_pain true never lowers acuity) is VIOLATED by the interaction_drop rule, which has the highest priority (100) and outputs the lowest acuity value (self_care) precisely when back_pain is added.

Counterexample fixing focal_weakness=true, abdominal_pain=false and flipping back_pain:

BEFORE (back_pain=false): raise_abdominal_pain not matched; raise_focal_weakness matched (priority 2) -> urgent; interaction_drop not matched (back_pain false). Highest-priority matching rule = raise_focal_weakness => acuity = urgent.

AFTER (back_pain=true): raise_abdominal_pain not matched; raise_focal_weakness matched (priority 2) -> urgent; interaction_drop matched, focal_weakness AND back_pain (priority 100) -> self_care. Highest priority wins => acuity = self_care.

Enum order self_care < routine < urgent < emergent, so acuity dropped from urgent to self_care when back_pain was set true. That lowers acuity, violating monotonicity. The witness assignment given is the post-flip input (focal_weakness=true, back_pain=true, abdominal_pain=false) yielding self_care, versus the same input with back_pain=false yielding urgent. (Note: the abdominal_pain=true variant is an even more dramatic violation: emergent -> self_care.)
